channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead Tree, 11th March
item.description:
Tree is dead outside property and branches falling causing hazard JW
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Raymond Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 11th March
item.description:
At the top of Leigh Hunt Drive people stand and toss their rubbish in the bushes and foliage. Its disgusting. Street cleaners never clean it. The problem is ongoing. A bin needs to be placed there and put on the collection route. Or a camera needs to put there so you can fine people. Its a complete lack of respect for our environment.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): High Street, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in the alleyway, 11th March
item.description:
Rubbish dumped in the alleyway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beal Close, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped on the pavement, 11th March
item.description:
Rubbish dumped on the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Darenth Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping & Rubbish on pavement, 11th March
item.description:
Once more rubbish and litter all over the pavement in front of 67 Queen's Drive. Including an exploded bin bag which is disgusting and unhygienic.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queens Drive, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Serious Fly-Tipping Issue – Request for Council Action, 11th March
item.description:
There is constant fly-tipping outside our property. People regularly leave items such as mattresses, bags of rubbish, baby strollers, and other waste. They also keep putting their rubbish in our bins, which fills them up and leaves no space for our own waste. Because of this, we had to move our two bins inside the property. However, people are still leaving rubbish in the space where the bins used to be. We are very tired of having to clean up other people’s litter all the time. We tried calling the council’s fly-tipping service, but no one answered, so we are writing this email instead. We pay £185 every month in council tax, yet we are the ones constantly cleaning up other people’s rubbish. Can someone please take this issue seriously? This is not a one-time problem—it keeps happening again and again, and we are extremely frustrated and exhausted dealing with it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Riverdale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Balloon Gas Canisters Dumped, 11th March
item.description:
There's a bunch of these dumped on our streets. Also on corner Ronaldstone and Fen Grove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Fen Grove, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water coming from under metal cover and running into street, 11th March
item.description:
Water is flowing from under the triangular shaped metal cover and running into the road for a week or more.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mentmore Terrace, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Balloon Gas Canisters Dumped, 11th March
item.description:
There's a bunch of these cannisters dropped in our area. Also on Raeburn Road. I'll send a separate report for that.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Raeburn Road, Falconwood, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pile of e-bikes blocking pavement near Sainsbury's Local, 11th March
item.description:
There is a pile of bikes (mostly Lime but also 1 Forest bike) on the pavement at Queenstown Road just prior to the entrance to the Sainsbury's Local store (646-650 Wandsworth Road). I could not safely access the numbers to share. As a person with limited mobility (using crutches) I was unable to use the pavement to reach my destination or access the store. I had therefore to turn around and make a much longer journey, by bus to Sainsbury's at Clapham Junction.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queenstown Road, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 11th March
item.description:
Overflowing bin to be emptied please. Located opposite Crayford station entrance on the Station Road approach walkway - thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stadium Way, Crayford, Dartford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is a dumped car outside our house., 11th March
item.description:
Someone dumped a car outside our house on the 9th March (Monday). He came along in a flat bed and lifted it off with a crane. Nobody along the street knows who it is. It is an eyesore and is taking up space. Also, there are some road resurfacing works going on and it will stop them from completing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Drive, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bikes causing obstruction in road and residents parking bay please remove, 11th March
item.description:
Bikes causing obstruction in road and residents parking bay please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ainger Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farnaby Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
